text:
Once upon a time in Meadowville, there was a young girl named Eleanor who loved collecting butterflies. She spent afternoons in the forest, hidden away behind the antique bookstore her grandmother ran. Every spring, the forest was abuzz with various butterflies, but Eleanor’s favorites were the cheerful goldlines.

One sunny afternoon, while searching for patches of wildflowers that might have caterpillars, Eleanor stumbled upon an old oak tree. At the base of the trunk was a peculiar, dusty box. Curiosity getting the better of her, Eleanor pulled it open. Inside was an antique looking glass, but peculiar. It wasn’t just any glass; it had silver edges, showing images of butterflies.

As Eleanor looked into it, a delicate butterfly popped into view. Its wings shimmered like moonlight. She scooped it up. Returning to her home, she carefully set the butterfly onto a leaf. She studied it every day, naming it Charlotte.

One day, Charlotte became invisible. Eleanor searched
